stem: A public health study surveys 15 residents surveyed and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 15 (26.7%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (10 observations, rate = 10.0%) and Group B (5 observations, rate = 60.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 43% and 59% of the population. Estimate the true vaccination coverage and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2667. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.3695 (range [0.3333, 0.4056]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.109, 0.5195]. Point estimate: 0.3695.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.51: 0.51×0.148 + 0.49×0.6 = 0.3695.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
